_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for Micron Technology (MU). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Wisesheets — not yet pulled. Agent must search for TTM financials before running DCF.
_cycle_note: Use cycle-normalized revenue as base, not peak. FY2023 trough was $15.5B. Current cycle peak ~$38–40B. Normalized mid-cycle ~$25–28B.
story_narrative: Micron's HBM inflection thesis: HBM earns 5–8x standard DRAM ASP on contracted (not spot) pricing. Micron secured NVIDIA Blackwell HBM3E design win — #2 in HBM after SK Hynix. If HBM grows to 30–40% of revenue mix, it structurally raises the margin floor and dampens the severity of future DRAM troughs. FY2023 showed what commodity DRAM alone looks like — $5.8B operating loss. The question is whether HBM is large enough (~10–15% of bit output but 40–50% of revenue at peak) to prevent recurrence. CXMT (China) commoditizing legacy DRAM is a real threat; the thesis holds only if Micron stays in HBM and continues to close on SK Hynix.
story_revenue_cagr: 20% reflects HBM structural uplift + DRAM recovery cycle from normalized base (~$25B). Bear case 10–12% if HBM ramp stalls or CXMT accelerates commodity cannibalization.
story_target_margin: 38% assumes HBM premium mix at full ramp. Current cycle peak is 47%+ but cycle-normalized is ~25–30%. 38% is the optimistic mid-case where HBM structurally raises the floor.
story_growth_years: 10 years — HBM ramp + next memory generation (HBM4). DRAM cycles typically 3–4 years; 10 years captures 2–3 cycles with structural HBM shift.
story_stable_growth: 3% = global memory market long-run growth. Memory is more commoditized than logic; stable growth can't exceed GDP meaningfully.
story_stable_margin: 28% reflects a future where HBM is 30–40% of revenue at structurally higher margins. Conservative assumption versus current peak.
story_s2c_high: 0.7x — memory is highly capital intensive. TSMC-equivalent spending: Micron builds its own fabs. CHIPS Act reduces some burden but new fabs ($10B+) are expensive.
story_s2c_stable: 0.8x — slight improvement as HBM fabs reach full utilization and incremental capacity needs less greenfield spend.
story_beta: 1.3 — higher than foundry beta reflects commodity DRAM cycle volatility. DRAM cycles can cut stock price 60–70% trough-to-trough.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization. Memory process node IP (1-beta DRAM, HBM stacking) has useful life of ~5 years before the next node supersedes.
story_uncertainty: High — commodity cycle adds severe earnings volatility, CXMT threat is real, and timing the cycle is nearly impossible. Wide MoS required before buying.
